slides.title: A short walk *after meals* changes everything.
slides.sub: It isn't about more steps. It's about timing. A gentle walk after eating steadies your blood sugar.

slides.title: Eating raises blood sugar
slides.body: Every meal sends a wave of glucose into your blood. How smoothly your body handles it *matters* for aging well.

slides.title: Ten minutes blunts the rise
slides.body: Walking soon after eating helps your muscles use that glucose. A short stroll *may help* soften the post-meal spike.

slides.title: Timing beats intensity here
slides.body: You don't need a hard workout. A relaxed walk in the right window *does more* than the same walk hours later.

slides.title: Make it a ritual
slides.body: Tie the walk to a meal you eat daily. Pairing it with dinner *can turn* a tip into an effortless routine.

slides.title: Walk a little, often
slides.body: A few quiet minutes after meals can support steadier energy and healthier aging. Small, well timed, repeatable.

slides.title: Turn meals into *easy wins*.
slides.body: Vibe Health folds your movement, food and steps into one daily score, so simple habits like a post-meal walk add up.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The secret isn't more steps. It's when you take them. 🌿

A meal sends glucose into your blood, and a gentle ten-minute walk soon after may help blunt the rise. Timing beats intensity. Tie it to dinner and it sticks. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns small, well-timed habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How a Short Walk After Meals Steadies Your Blood Sugar
